detectedChampions.improvement: Mistake: You stayed too far forward in lane and did not respect the enemy Teleport coming in while exposed. recordings.title: Drake secure became one more death window
recordings.feedbackTitle: Drake secure became one more death window
recordings.feedback: The leak is turning a finished dragon win into one more brush fight, so secured objective value becomes dead time instead of reset, mid wave, or Baron setup.
recordings.gameDetail: At 24:50, Hextech Drake is already secured, your team is grouped on the bot-river exit, and the legal value left is leave through river, catch the next wave, or reset, so the wrong click is dashing deeper into the brush fight and the next click is walk out with allies, spend mid, or base for the next setup. By 26:51 you are on death screen, which matters because the extra fight deletes the dragon payout and hands back tempo that should have become map control.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Rep: after every objective fight, ask: did we already get the value? If yes, choose dragon, wave, recall, or group. Do not take a second fight while low or unsupported unless an ally is still in front and the enemy is already trapped or low.
recordings.eventEvidence: 10:15 shows a stable bot reset state with wave and support; 22:50 shows a legal Baron setup with allies grouped; 24:50 shows Hextech Drake already secured while Samira is still fighting in the river choke; 26:51 shows the death screen after the extra re-fight.
recordings.failureEvidence: At 24:50 the objective is already won and allies are positioned to leave with the dragon payout, but staying in the river brush fight turns a finished secure into the 26:51 death timer instead of wave, reset, or the next neutral setup.
recordings.goodThing: The setup before the throw is real: the 22:50 Baron posture is patient and grouped, and the 24:50 drake secure shows you can arrive to an objective with allies instead of face-checking alone; keep that first entry shape.
recordings.whyTrust: This uses inspected 10:15, 22:50, 24:50, and 26:51 frames from the same recorded match, so the coaching is tied to one visible post-objective re-entry mistake rather than a generic Samira rule.
recordings.evidence: Manual frame inspection of the grouped objective setup, drake secure, extra river fight, and death-screen consequence from the same match.
recordings.pattern: The game is not failing on the first setup. The legal group entry is there, but once the objective is already secured the click stays in fight mode too long, so won value keeps getting converted back into risk instead of tempo.
recordings.diamondRule: After dragon is secured, Samira's next click is leave with allies, catch wave, or reset; a forward brush re-entry is illegal unless an ally still fronts the screen and the target is already trapped or low.
recordings.drill: after every objective fight, ask: did we already get the value? If yes, choose dragon, wave, recall, or group. Do not take a second fight while low or unsupported unless an ally is still in front and the enemy is already trapped or low.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Bot siege became a jungle chase
recordings.feedback: The leak is that a winning bot siege keeps turning into one more chase off the tower line, so free structure tempo becomes low-value jungle fighting and another avoidable death window.
recordings.gameDetail: At 9:58, you have bot wave under enemy tower with an ally beside you and the defender already backing off, so the wrong click is following the retreat path into red-side jungle and the next click is hit the free tower, clear the wave, or reset behind ally cover. By 10:34 you are low in enemy jungle instead of spending the bot-side value cleanly. The Co-op AI clip still finishes as a 20/4/3, 117 CS win, about 5. The leak is that the won or playable moment turns back into enemy tempo before your lead becomes a permanent map result.

recordings.title: Losing fights need a next-click script
recordings.feedbackTitle: Losing fights need a next-click script
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you kept playing messy or losing states as if one more fight would fix them, even when enemies were alive, your health was dropping, or teammates were dead. Fix: at the mistake timestamp, back out, clear only the safe wave, or wait behind the first ally before entering again.
recordings.gameDetail: At 23:33, Samira behind a Baron-powered mid push with multiple enemy defenders still alive, so the wrong click is continuing the old task after the visible state changed and the next click is to choose the visible result now: structure, wave, recall, group, or one step back if none is safe. By 23:43 you are dead with multiple allies also down, so the leak is not that you lacked damage; the leak is that the fight continued after the safe damage window ended and gave the enemy a clean push back. At 24:20, when you respawn with several teammates still dead, do not run out looking for a fight or a side route; hold base, clear the closest wave under turret, and wait for at least two teammates to spawn. At 26:54 the same rule appears again with four allies dead: stop at the base/inhibitor line, do not walk up bottom jungle or lane, and only clear minions that enter tower range. The simple next-game script is fight from behind a body while the state is even, and when the state is losing, defend one safe wave until teammates are alive.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Rep: after every objective fight, ask: did we already get the value? If yes, choose dragon, wave, recall, or group. Do not take a second fight while low or unsupported unless an ally is still in front and the enemy is already CC'd or low.
recordings.eventEvidence: 23:33 shows Samira behind a Baron-powered mid push with multiple enemy defenders still alive; 23:43 shows Samira dead after the fight continues; 24:20 shows Samira respawning while several teammates are still dead; 26:54 shows Samira moving out while four allies are dead.
recordings.failureEvidence: At 23:33 the fight is already crowded with enemy defenders alive, so the safe damage window is ending. By 23:43 Samira is dead with allies also down; at 24:20 and 26:54 the visible failure repeats as forward movement starts while teammates are still dead instead of holding the base line.
recordings.goodThing: At 19:52 you are in the better shape: allies are in front of Samira near mid, so you can damage from behind them instead of being the first body enemies reach.
recordings.whyTrust: The review is tied to the death-state frames: the game was already hard, so the fastest improvement is removing the extra deaths after the safe fight window is gone.
recordings.evidence: 23:33 shows Samira behind a Baron-powered mid push with multiple enemy defenders still alive; 23:43 shows Samira dead after the fight continues; 24:20 shows Samira respawning while several teammates are still dead; 26:54 shows Samira moving out while four allies are dead.
recordings.pattern: The repeated mistake is trying to solve a losing state by walking forward or staying in the fight. The higher-value habit is to switch from carry mode to damage-control mode the moment allies are dead, enemy tools are still available, or your HP no longer lets you survive a re-engage.
recordings.diamondRule: When the state is losing, your next click is not hero damage; it is safe wave, base line, or wait for bodies.
recordings.drill: Next game, say even or losing before every mid-game fight; if the answer is losing, your only allowed clicks are back, tower wave, or wait.

recordings.title: Passive Level 1 Botlane (Remake Situation)
recordings.feedbackTitle: Passive Level 1 Botlane (Remake Situation)
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You did not contest early lane state or pressure for level 2 when the enemy botlane was present and your support was AFK, conceding full wave and lane control. Fix: Auto-pilot AFK waits lose you both pressure and tempo in every game; instead, if your support is absent, focus on maximizing safe XP soak without ceding full control, using tower cover to grab as much gold/XP as possible or threaten with your range, and be alert for remake with minimal risk.
recordings.gameDetail: At 01:05 in botlane, enemy Nautilus and Senna arrive first to lane while your Karma is AFK in fountain/base, as seen on minimap and remake bar. You remain far back, not contesting any last hits or threatening from bush/under-tower, which hands complete wave control to Nautilus/Senna and will snowball lane state against you in any non-remake scenario. Level 1 Samira is weak, but leaving two champions uncontested guarantees they get prio, crash, and zone you—especially critical versus a ranged + engaged support where staying in XP range with minimal risk is mandatory. Challenger-level botlaners maximize whatever XP, single last hits, and bush vision they can grab even in a lost 2v1, because ceding 1-2 minutes of lane XP is unrecoverable. Around 1:04, at and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested; By 1:16, at and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested. Even in remake games, build the discipline to claim auto-range last hits and punish enemy over-aggression under turret to prevent permanent low-tempo habits from forming.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Lane posturing and A-click/camera discipline—your camera and positioning snap to max distance from Nautilus/Senna with zero check of their spellcast animations or fog threat; in future games, hold camera at spell range and A-click toward melee minions just out of hook range to pressure every possible XP safely, even at low FPS.
recordings.eventEvidence: 1:04 At and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested; 1:16 At and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested
recordings.goodThing: You do not greed forward or die to a likely Nautilus hook/trap in a doomed 1v2.
recordings.whyTrust: In Challenger bot lanes, players denied from level 2 lose all ability to control crash, XP, and safe farm for 5+ minutes; claiming wave XP and exerting even minimal threat is the only way to prevent snowball disasters.
recordings.evidence: 1:04 At and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested; 1:16 At and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested
recordings.pattern: The main issue is complete surrender of lane presence in AFK support/remake scenarios, with no attempt to maximize XP or contest single last hits. Camera stays far back with no spell-range active threat checks, and no defensive posture for recoverable XP.
recordings.diamondRule: Never surrender full wave XP and pressure without at least testing safe last hits or auto-range trade windows, especially versus engage supports—every uncontested push doubles the lane bleed.
recordings.drill: Next time support is AFK or lane is lost, hold tower and auto-range for as many minions/XP ticks as safely possible, using camera toggle and A-click to check for engages.

recordings.title: Failed to Rotate Off Sidelane to Pressure Mid
recordings.feedbackTitle: Failed to Rotate Off Sidelane to Pressure Mid
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You stayed bot and continued farming long after the bot turret fell and your team grouped for mid/inhib plays. Fix: After first turret and the wave is past river, you must rotate to mid to pressure with your team and threaten objectives instead of leaking time farming bot alone.
recordings.gameDetail: Around 24:28, after (frame 6) you and your enchanter crash down the last hits on bot outer with all dragon-side objectives cleared and enemy pressure breaking, but you stay bot side for multiple minutes in the following frames, chasing bot wave and jungle instead of rotating mid where your teammates group for a heavy push (frame 10, clock visible); this is the start or nearest visible start of the main mistake window; earlier at 13:15, from onward, bot turret is gone, wave is pushed, team groups mid, but Samira is caught farming bot and hovering alone with no dragon or tower to take; earlier at 21:21, Samira in the middle of a recall at outer bot turret, ally lane minions and enemies nearby. Grouped mid is better only when the visible state supports it, because mid is the shortest lane to towers/base, allies can stand between Samira and the collapse, and enemies have to defend structure instead of chasing through fog. This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Fix late recalls and wasted time low in lane—when low HP and after fighting or objective, recall immediately rather than half-heartedly farming dead waves; next game, recall as soon as your productive play is over or you’re at risk.
recordings.eventEvidence: 13:15 shows from onward, bot turret is gone, wave is pushed, team groups mid, but Samira is caught farming bot and hovering alone with no dragon or tower to take. 21:21 shows Samira in the middle of a recall at outer bot turret, ally lane minions and enemies nearby. 24:28 shows after (frame 6) you and your enchanter crash down the last hits on bot outer with all dragon-side objectives cleared and enemy pressure breaking, but you stay bot side for multiple minutes in the following frames, chasing bot wave and jungle instead of rotating mid where your teammates group for a heavy push (frame 10, clock visible).
recordings.goodThing: You correctly converted early bot tower pressure with support backup and did not greed-dive post-tower, showing some conversion discipline.
recordings.whyTrust: This feedback tracks with visible team groupings, minimap rotations, and open structures: not rotating to mid means you miss the team snowball timing and risk solo deaths when you are needed to punch through mid or defend; this is a repeatable core ADC habit at all ranks.
recordings.evidence: 13:15 From onward, bot turret is gone, wave is pushed, team groups mid, but Samira is caught farming bot and hovering alone with no dragon or tower to take. 21:21 Samira in the middle of a recall at outer bot turret, ally lane minions and enemies nearby. 24:28 After (frame 6) you and your enchanter crash down the last hits on bot outer with all dragon-side objectives cleared and enemy pressure breaking, but you stay bot side for multiple minutes in the following frames, chasing bot wave and jungle instead of rotating mid where your teammates group for a heavy push (frame 10, clock visible).
recordings.pattern: After securing bot tower with help, you stay bot side and farm past lane expiration, instead of joining team for mid pressure and conversion. This leaks your threat window and delays your team’s ability to break open the map.
recordings.diamondRule: Never stay in dead lane solo after tower when teammates are grouping mid—rotate through mid and show for team pressure or objectives.
recordings.drill: In your next 3 games, after taking bot outer, push wave past river then hard-rotate mid at the next recall or lane reset and group for at least two mid waves and one turret push.

recordings.feedback: Mistake: You stayed bot after the lane had no clean payout, so your time turned into isolated side pressure instead of a reset or map rotation. Fix: Once bot outer is gone and the wave is not a free turret or dragon setup, reset or move through safe vision toward mid/team.
recordings.gameDetail: Around 15:55, Samira is still pushing the side lane toward enemy turret after earlier bot pressure at 9:44 and 14:37; this is the nearest visible start of the main mistake window because the lane is no longer giving an immediate tower, dive, or dragon setup. The leak is time and safety: staying side makes your next useful arrival later and exposes Samira to a collapse, while resetting or moving through vision lets your gold and damage arrive where the next fight or objective will happen. Keep the good habit from 9:44 and 14:37, where you respected pressure under tower, but after the lane stops paying, call reset or rotate instead of farming one more dead wave.

recordings.goodThing: Had the discipline earlier to back up under tower instead of dying when enemies pressured.
recordings.whyTrust: This is a repeat soloQ pattern—when a lost side lane stays contested, you give up team plays and get picked or lose vision/objectives; Samira especially loses value without numbers or mid control.
recordings.evidence: 9:44 Samira stands under bottom outer turret defending against two approaching enemies and minions. 14:37 Samira under bottom lane turret with an ally, two enemies approaching from lane. 15:55 Samira pushing minion wave in side lane toward enemy turret.

recordings.drill: Next game, when side outer falls, immediately recall and show up mid or at the closest contestable wave unless a dive/farm is guaranteed and safe.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Low-HP Overstay After Wins
recordings.feedback: Mistake: After winning trades or securing kills, you stay too long with low HP and no clear next payout, risking enemy return and losing reset tempo. Fix: Recall and spend after a successful fight or turret unless there is a guaranteed safe tower hit or protected wave, especially when HP is low or enemy spawns are up.
recordings.gameDetail: Around 0:50, Samira is in the bot lane with the first minion wave, positioned close to her own tower, uncontested by enemy champions; By 1:29, clear evidence for the overstaying lesson: Samira is low HP after winning a fight and lingers in lane instead of recalling to reset and spend gold, showing the risk of enemy return and loss of tempo; Then around 3:06, Samira is mid-fight in bot lane, shielded and actively auto-attacking an enemy amid a large minion wave. This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.

recordings.title: Base break became a delayed sync
recordings.feedbackTitle: Base break became a delayed sync
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after the base was cracked, you let the next minute become mid-wave cleanup and a late rejoin instead of an immediate finish-or-reset call. Fix: once the inhibitor/nexus area opens, say finish, reset, or sync next wave before touching another mid wave.
recordings.gameDetail: At 11:12, the base already cracked with Samira and allies inside, so the wrong click is stepping forward after the safe payout disappeared and the next click is to choose the visible result now: structure, wave, recall, group, or one step back if none is safe. At 11:27 you correctly fight with allies in the base and get the takedown instead of chasing somewhere useless. At 11:47 the next decision point appears: the tower is down, you have about 3034 gold, minions and allies are still in the base, and the clean Master-climb call is either finish now or reset on the same timing as the team. At 12:32 and 12:42 you are back mid collecting waves after spending, which means the old reset critique was too vague; the actual leak is that the next base hit is no longer clearly synced. By 12:52 your team is fighting again near the opened base while you are still arriving from mid, and at 13:00 the rejoin works in this game, but stronger teams use that delay to clear waves, respawn, or collapse on the late carry. The lesson is: after base breaks, do not let a correct reset turn into a disconnected re-entry.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Rep: in every base push, say structure, blocker, wave, or exit before the forward click; hit the structure if free, hit only the blocker if safe, otherwise leave.
recordings.eventEvidence: 11:12 shows the base already cracked with Samira and allies inside; 11:27 shows the base fight working with allies; 11:47 shows about 3034 gold and a fresh tower-down window; 12:32 to 12:42 shows mid-wave cleanup after spending; 12:52 to 13:00 shows the late rejoin into the next base fight.
recordings.failureEvidence: At 11:12, the base already cracked with Samira and allies inside; the failure is staying in pressure mode after the safe payout is no longer proven, which leaks time, safety, or defense and gives the enemy another fight or push window.
recordings.goodThing: You did create real base pressure with the team and appear to spend before the next mid wave; the fix is the re-entry timing after that, not pretending the whole sequence was bad.
recordings.whyTrust: The evidence changes the diagnosis: visible gold drops after the base break, so the mistake is not simply refusing to spend; it is losing team sync on the next push.
recordings.evidence: 11:12 shows the base already cracked with Samira and allies inside; 11:27 shows the base fight working with allies; 11:47 shows about 3034 gold and a fresh tower-down window; 12:32 to 12:42 shows mid-wave cleanup after spending; 12:52 to 13:00 shows the late rejoin into the next base fight.
recordings.pattern: The repeated climb leak is not only chasing; it is letting a won base state turn into an unclear next state. Once the base is open, every next action should preserve finish pressure, reset value, or team sync.
recordings.diamondRule: After a base break, the next wave only matters if it syncs the next base hit.
recordings.drill: After inhibitor/base pressure, call one word before the next click: finish, reset, or sync.

recordings.feedback: Mistake: Stayed bot lane with low HP and high gold after winning fight and clearing wave. Fix: Immediately recall to reset after the kill and wave clear to spend gold and avoid dying to respawned opponents.

This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.
recordings.eventEvidence: 0:55 Samira is moving alone through the bottom lane near her tier 2 tower as minions have just spawned, with vision provided only in close proximity and the lane state not yet established. 0:57 Blue team bot laners are approaching the wave as enemy Anivia meets minions near bottom lane. 1:32 Samira is under her own tower with a moderate-sized enemy minion wave approaching
recordings.goodThing: Secured a winning trade and converted for a solo kill under threat.

recordings.evidence: 0:55 Samira is moving alone through the bottom lane near her tier 2 tower as minions have just spawned, with vision provided only in close proximity and the lane state not yet established. 0:57 Blue team bot laners are approaching the wave as enemy Anivia meets minions near bottom lane. 1:32 Samira is under her own tower with a moderate-sized enemy minion wave approaching

recordings.title: Finish-window all-in
recordings.feedbackTitle: Finish-window all-in
recordings.feedback: Mistake: over-learning this clip would make you throw; the same dash is bad if it starts without wave, teammate body, or nexus pressure. Fix: full send only when every reset moves toward the payout.
recordings.gameDetail: At 14:21 the fight is already in their base, not in river or a side lane: allies and minions are in front, enemy defenders are packed near nexus, and Samira is close enough to chain resets without walking into fog. At 14:27 the first two kills land and the play becomes a real cleanup instead of a random chase. At 14:33 the chain reaches triple, at 14:38 the quadra happens beside the nexus area, and at 14:41 the penta lands because the fight never leaves the ending window. Learn this exactly: the all-in is good when the map is already ending; outside that condition, take tower, wave, objective, or recall first.
recordings.eventEvidence: 14:21 fight starts inside the enemy base with multiple enemy defenders still alive; 14:27 double kill; 14:33 triple; 14:38 quadra near nexus; 14:41 penta as the base remains open.
recordings.goodThing: The strong part is the finish discipline: once the enemy line collapses, Samira stays with the ally/minion push and cleans through the defenders instead of drifting sideways.
recordings.whyTrust: The penta is not random: the clip shows the conditions that made it valid, with allies and minions already in base and every reset moving toward nexus.
recordings.evidence: Manual storyboard review of the May 20 highlight: visible base fight from 14:21 to 14:41, double/triple/quadra/penta sequence, and enemy base already open.
recordings.pattern: Samira's best-looking fight here works because the map already gives the exit and the payout. The dangerous habit would be copying the same depth when the fight is not already a base finish.
recordings.diamondRule: All-in only when the reset path and the payout are the same direction.
recordings.drill: Before E/R, ask: does every reset move me toward nexus, tower, dragon, Baron, or safety?

recordings.feedback: Mistake: you chase sideways after the base is already open. Fix: stand behind the first body in and hit the structure as soon as space is made.
recordings.gameDetail: At 12:10, low Samira below the open enemy base entrance, so the wrong click is continuing the old task after the visible state changed and the next click is to choose the visible result now: structure, wave, recall, group, or one step back if none is safe. Around 13:05 the map slows down: champion walks away mid, an ally is channeling recall, and Samira is full HP with the lane already open, which is exactly the moment to choose wave/base route before another target appears. Around 13:15 the camera follows champion again and Samira casts Q/Flair into him from close range; the spell connects, but the leak is that the fight is sideways while the open base is still the real payout. Around 14:19 the clean version finally shows up: multiple enemies are dead, minions are in the base, Samira is behind the front body, and the team is hitting the ending area instead of wandering after another duel. The lesson is to treat the open base as the destination, because structure damage ends the game while side duels only reopen collapse windows.

recordings.pattern: The winning pressure was already available. The mistake is treating a nearby Samira like the win condition after the actual win condition is already open.

recordings.feedback: Mistake: you keep playing for another fight after the first win. Fix: take wave, tower, reset, or end before touching another Samira.
recordings.gameDetail: Honest read: this is improved, but the same leak keeps coming back after the good moment. Around 1:24 Samira is fighting under the friendly bot tower with Jinx and Braum still on screen; the movement is forward into a contested lane pocket while the safer value is to thin the wave, hold the tower line, and make them walk into you. Around 7:58 the good version happens: the bot push is already under enemy tower, the wave is with you, and the won pressure turns into turret damage instead of another random chase. Around 10:32 Samira is low in bot lane while Braum and Jinx can still answer, so every extra step forward is a shutdown invitation unless the next click is reset or structure. The lesson is not to fight less; it is to make the first won fight buy the wave, turret, reset, or end before accepting the second one.

recordings.title: You stay when one hit kills you
recordings.feedbackTitle: You stay when one hit kills you
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you keep playing the wave when one auto or spell kills you. Fix: give the wave and recall before the lane turns into a death timer.
recordings.gameDetail: Honest read: this is not a mechanics issue; it is a health-gate issue. At 2:43 Samira is under bot tower with the enemy wave arriving, Ashe still visible to the right, and enough missing HP that the lane should already be treated as dangerous instead of playable. At 3:27 the real mistake is clearer: Samira is still beside the bot wave at lethal health, the camera and movement are staying with minions, and the recall path has not been taken even though one clean enemy touch can turn the wave into a death timer. At 4:57 the good version is visible after the reset: Samira is back on the map with enough HP to walk forward, Q/auto from range, and choose the next trade instead of begging not to be hit. By 14:28 the team is ending with Samira alive around the base fight, which proves the champion damage is not the blocker. The blocker is staying in lane after the health bar says the next decision should be recall.
recordings.eventEvidence: 2:43 Samira is under bot tower with Ashe still visible and the wave arriving; 3:27 Samira is still in lane at lethal health; 4:57 reset gives enough HP to re-enter; 14:28 the later team ending works.
recordings.goodThing: After the bad early health decision, you still got back into the game and converted later pressure into the ending push.
recordings.whyTrust: This is not vague advice: the reviewed frames show the exact one-hit-health lane stay, and removing that habit protects the aggression that already works later.
recordings.evidence: Manual storyboard review of the May 18 full recording: early lane death at lethal HP, later kills and turret/base conversion after grouping.
recordings.pattern: The new game is better at converting once Samira has items, but the early lane still has a lethal-HP greed point: staying for the wave while one hit from death turns a manageable recall into a death timer.
recordings.diamondRule: Below one enemy auto or spell, the wave is no longer the objective; reset first, then play the next item spike.
recordings.drill: At low HP, say one hit kills me and recall unless the enemy bot lane is dead or fully gone.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Respect missing and incoming TPs
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You stayed too far forward in lane and did not respect the enemy Teleport coming in while exposed. Fix: When your ADC is hitting the wave and the enemy TP is coming down, immediately back off from melee range and give ground until you confirm numbers and threat.
recordings.gameDetail: The wave was pushing forward with Caitlyn present and Samira in melee range of the enemy caster minions, with both Caitlyn and Samira pushing just past river. A Teleport appears directly into the lane, yet Samira does not back off; she is caught in the middle of the wave and immediately crowd-controlled and killed, giving a shutdown. The better response is to immediately pull back as soon as TP is channeled, especially when ahead and holding a shutdown, completely avoiding the play. Around 3:34, blue team pushes bottom lane with minions near enemy turret; By 3:37, red team Samira teleports in, blue Samira is stunned in bot lane fight; Then around 3:40, Samira is killed for a shutdown, showing the consequence of staying too far forward and not respecting the incoming Teleport. Always respect missing enemies and incoming Teleports when you are far up in lane.
recordings.eventEvidence: 3:34 Blue team pushes bottom lane with minions near enemy turret; 3:37 Red team Samira teleports in, blue Samira is stunned in bot lane fight; 3:40 Samira is killed for a shutdown—shows the consequence of staying too far forward and not respecting the incoming Teleport, supporting the lesson on respecting missing enemies and incoming TPs.
recordings.goodThing: You and your lane partner pushed the wave aggressively to pressure the enemy tower.
recordings.whyTrust: Getting caught by TP like this instantly swings tempo and gold back to the enemy—this is a punishable, avoidable error and safe habits here directly stop throwing leads.
recordings.evidence: 3:34 Blue team pushes bottom lane with minions near enemy turret; 3:37 Red team Samira teleports in, blue Samira is stunned in bot lane fight; 3:40 Samira is killed for a shutdown—shows the consequence of staying too far forward and not respecting the incoming Teleport, supporting the lesson on respecting missing enemies and incoming TPs.
recordings.pattern: Positioning aggressively in lane with a shutdown lead but not reacting to cross-map threats or Teleports results in immediate punish; the kill was 100% avoidable with minimal discipline.
recordings.diamondRule: If you see a Teleport coming in near you and you don't have numbers, back off instantly—never greed wave or aggression with shutdown gold exposed.
recordings.drill: In your next game, back away instantly on every enemy Teleport in your lane, even if you think they might not have kill pressure—build the habit of never greeting TP plays.

recordings.title: Reset after the turret falls
recordings.feedbackTitle: Reset after the turret falls
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you are dead as the red turret falls, so the objective win turns into a stagger instead of a clean reset. Fix: once the turret drops, exit with the wave or recall with the team before the next enemy bodies arrive.
recordings.gameDetail: The objective went through, so the good part is that the team got structure value; the leak is dying during or right after that conversion, which removes your ability to reset, spend, and help the next map play. The better decision after the turret falls is exit or recall, not staying in the lane long enough to become the stagger. The lesson is to make the objective win end the sequence cleanly.
recordings.goodThing: Your team broke the inhibitor turret, creating an opportunity for a powerful map reset or further pressure.
recordings.whyTrust: This death costs Samira both tempo and agency on the map, but the correct reset after a turret break directly leads to more vision and better drag/baron prep in every rank.
recordings.pattern: The structure conversion happened, but the death means the sequence did not end cleanly; the next improvement is exiting after the payout.
recordings.diamondRule: When the turret falls, the sequence is over unless the next structure is free right now.
recordings.drill: After every turret take, click back once and check recall before looking for another fight.
nuance: Good: the team still took the turret.
Leak: Samira was dead during the payout instead of resetting after it.
Consequence: the structure win becomes a stagger, so the next objective setup is weaker.
Next check: turret falls, exit or recall unless the next structure is free immediately.
recordings.reviewLimit: This short clip has one reviewable frame, so it supports the turret-death timing but not the full fight setup.
